candidates.title: Modernized InDesign Catalogue (Massey Harpers Game & Country)
candidates.tools: InDesign · Photoshop — Master-page template system, multi-page A4 booklet and print-ready PDF — explicitly requires .indd deliverables.
candidates.brief: Design a product catalogue and template pages for Massey Harpers Game and Country in Adobe InDesign. Deliverables: a multi-page printed A4 booklet and downloadable PDF with reusable page designs. Front cover requires a full-page hero image with company logo, catalogue name and year. Back cover needs a full-page image with logo and contact details (phone, email, website, physical address). Informational pages must include a contents list with page numbers, company biography, detailed contact information and terms/conditions space. Feed-page design should follow the existing format with space for 2–4 logos, a feed-guide table, and a feed-experts section (expert image, name, phone, email, job title, plus notes/disclaimer space). Deliver a minimum of five different product/inside page designs for grid layouts, supporting banner ads, disclaimers, special offers, product ranges and full/half-p

candidates.title: Vibrant Social & Web Graphics
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork and video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I need a steady stream of bold, energetic visuals that tie every corner of my online presence together. The work centers on crafting platform-specific social media posts for Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, Threads, YouTube, and Pinterest, all in a lively, eye-catching style that sparks engagement. To extend that same energy, I also want matching website graphics—hero images, section headers, and call-to-action spots—plus occasional banner ads for promotional campaigns. Every asset should feel part of one cohesive family while being perfectly sized, compressed, and formatted for its destination. Deliverables • Editable source files (AI / PSD / Figma) for each design • Web-ready exports in the correct dimensions and file types • Mini style reference (colours, typography) to keep future assets consistent Acceptance criteria 1. Visually bold and vibrant, with clear hierarchy and conf

candidates.title: Brand identity for ordinary SAfricans who want to start, fix or grow their small business
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork and layered/retouched assets
candidates.brief: Our new platform will give aspiring and struggling South African entrepreneurs a single place to test ideas, get guidance and join live online events. To make that vision instantly credible, I need a visual identity anchored by an illustrative logo that radiates trust and reliability. Here is what I have in mind: the logo should be illustration-driven rather than purely typographic, flexible enough to sit on an app icon, event banners and social posts, while still feeling professional. I am intentionally not prescribing colours; surprise me with a palette that feels fresh to the South African market yet reinforces the sense of trust. Deliverables I will sign off on • Primary and secondary logo files in vector (AI/SVG) and web-ready PNG + JPEG • Square app icon adaptation • Simple brand guide covering colour codes, fonts and usage rules • Social header or hero graphic that shows the syste

candidates.title: Vibrant Social Media Assets
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork and layered/retouched assets
candidates.brief: I’m looking for a designer who work with us on monthly basis only and keep my social channels fresh, eye-catching, and on brand. Most of the work revolves around Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, and LinkedIn, with the email banner and short, lightweight animation capabilities. Here’s what typically lands on the desk: • Colorful, scroll-stopping static posts • Stories, Reels, and other vertical formats • Header graphics, event banners, and hero images • Simple motion pieces or GIF-style loops The brand you’ll be supporting sells tech products, so familiarity with that space—and the ability to translate complex ideas into friendly, visual bites—will be a big plus. I already use a mix of AI-assisted tools in my workflow (e.g., Leonardo, Claude tools), so if you’ve experimented with any of these, let me know; it’ll speed up collaboration. Files should arrive in editable format (PSD, AI, or laye

replacement_note: GEN-FREE replacement for a removed generative-expand task. NO image_generative_expand, NO generative fill/outpaint, NO upscale. O8 stock-hero = license a correctly-sized stock image + crop/reframe + place logo (NOT expand a small image).
